items.q: Did Gorbachev intend to dissolve the Soviet Union?
items.correct: No — he wanted to reform and save Soviet socialism; glasnost and perestroika unleashed forces (nationalism, economic chaos) he could not control.
items.misconception: Gorbachev took power planning to abolish communism and dissolve the USSR.
items.random_wrong: Gorbachev dissolved the USSR by signing a treaty with Ronald Reagan.
items.why: Gorbachev was a committed Communist reformer; the collapse was an unintended consequence. Students project the outcome backwards onto his intentions.

items.q: How was the Cuban Missile Crisis resolved?
items.correct: A secret deal: the USSR withdrew missiles from Cuba; the US publicly pledged not to invade Cuba and secretly agreed to remove Jupiter missiles from Turkey.
items.misconception: Kennedy stared Khrushchev down and the Soviets backed off unconditionally.
items.random_wrong: The crisis ended when Castro persuaded Khrushchev to withdraw.
items.why: The Turkey concession was kept secret for decades, fuelling the myth of unilateral US victory. The actual resolution was a negotiated compromise — important for understanding nuclear diplomacy.

items.q: What happened in the Russian Revolution of 1917?
items.correct: There were two revolutions: February overthrew the Tsar and installed the Provisional Government; October saw the Bolsheviks seize power from that government.
items.misconception: The Bolsheviks overthrew the Tsar in 1917.
items.random_wrong: Lenin personally led the storming of the Winter Palace.
items.why: Students collapse 1917 into one event. The Tsar abdicated in February under pressure from liberals and soldiers — the Bolsheviks took power eight months later from a republican government.

items.q: Roughly how much of the world's land did Britain rule by 1900?
items.why: It's A quarter. “A tenth” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which company ruled much of India before 1858?
items.why: Correct answer: East India Company. “Royal African” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did the British government take direct rule of India (start of the Raj)?
items.why: Exact answer: 1858. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
